name: Chroma
description: Open-source AI-native embedding database designed for LLM applications. Runs embedded or as a server; ideal for prototyping RAG systems locally with zero infrastructure.
curator_note: Fastest path from zero to working RAG prototype — no infra required.

name: Pinecone
description: Fully managed cloud vector database with single-digit millisecond query latency at billion-vector scale. First-class support for hybrid dense+sparse search.
curator_note: Go-to managed option when you need billion-scale search without ops overhead.

name: Qdrant
description: High-performance vector search engine written in Rust. Supports filtering, named vectors, and sparse vectors. Available as cloud or self-hosted with a gRPC/REST API.
curator_note: Best self-hosted option in 2026 — Rust performance with excellent filtering support.

name: Weaviate
description: Open-source vector database with built-in vectorization modules for text, images, and code. GraphQL API, multi-tenancy, and hybrid BM25+vector search out of the box.
curator_note: Unique built-in vectorizer modules make it ideal for multimodal RAG pipelines.

name: pgvector
description: Open-source PostgreSQL extension that adds vector similarity search. Store embeddings alongside relational data and query with standard SQL and ACID guarantees.
curator_note: If your data is already in Postgres, pgvector removes the need for a separate vector store.
